data.description: The Bank of Russia has extended the restrictions on cash withdrawals of foreign currency for another six months, until March 9, 2026.

data.title: Qualcomm & BMW introduce AI-enabled Snapdragon Ride system for automated driving, in BMW iX3. Validated in 60 countries, aiming for 100+ by 2026.
data.description: In San Diego, NASDAQ-listed QCOM has introduced the AI-powered Snapdragon Ride Pilot Automated Driving System at the IAA Mobility 2025 event in collaboration with the all-new BMW iX3. This system is driven by Snapdragon Ride system-on-chips and a newly created automated driving software stack. Validation for the system has been conducted across 60 countries globally, with plans to make it accessible in over 100 countries by 2026. The platform is scalable, allowing for the deployment of cost-effective single camera active safety systems, with the capability to expand to a multi-camera setup.

data.title: Global nuclear energy leaders at WNA Symposium warn of uranium shortage by 2030; demand rising sharply amid plans to triple capacity by 2050.
data.description: The World Nuclear Association hosted its 50th Symposium in London, where global nuclear energy leaders discussed the increasing electricity consumption by data centers. They predict that next year, data centers will consume as much electricity as Japan. Experts believe that tripling nuclear energy capacity by 2050 is necessary to meet demands, but a uranium shortage threatens this goal. The demand for fuel is rising sharply while supplies from existing mines are expected to halve between 2030 and 2040. The WNA is urging miners to speed up exploration and investment to prevent a potential crisis that could hinder the role of nuclear energy in meeting global energy needs. Additionally, the article mentions the rally of the VanEck Uranium ETF by 44% due to Trump's nuclear energy push and the pursuit of AI-powered energy solutions by companies like Microsoft, Google, and Nvidia.

data.title: INJ battles for backing at $12.69 amidst regulations, trading down 1.78%; potential breakout seen in Wave 3.
data.description: The stock symbol INJ is currently trading at $12.69, showing a decrease of 1.78% due to concerns regarding regulations, despite technical analysts identifying possible Wave 3 breakout patterns.

data.title: Bitcoin's recent drop follows a bear trap pattern from 2024, hinting at a major short squeeze in the coming weeks.
data.description: Bitcoin's recent decline from record highs may have underlying factors beyond surface appearance, potentially putting bear investors at risk in upcoming weeks.

data.title: Volunteers impacted by FSH Muscular Dystrophy rally for fundraising events across US and Canada in Sept-Oct 2025.
data.description: In September and October of 2025, the Walk & Roll to Cure FSHD will convene communities in the U.S. and Canada with the goal of raising funds and awareness for FSHD. The events bring together families, friends, and neighbors in Randolph, Mass. to support this cause.
